summary: Iteration 2 end-to-end testing for MIR Consulting (CMS + lead workflow + rate-limits + SEO). All 27 backend pytest cases pass. All critical frontend flows verified via Playwright: SEO (per-page titles, robots.txt 200, sitemap.xml 200), public Insights/CaseStudies fallback (4 placeholder cards each when DB empty), public real content rendering after publish, contact form submission with toast, admin login (wrong + correct), tabs switching, full Posts CRUD with live markdown preview, full Case Studies CRUD with outcomes, lead status drawer + notes + delete with confirm, and rate-limiting on /api/leads and /api/admin/login.

issues: Button label reads 'New case studie' (missing 'study'). Minor copy/grammar bug in /app/frontend/src/pages/Admin.jsx around the New <entity> button when entity name is singularized.

action_items: Cosmetic: fix the 'New case studie' button label on the Case Studies admin tab (should be 'New case study').
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py uses slowapi default key_func=get_remote_address which keys on the immediate peer (the ingress proxy). In a real public deployment all traffic shares one IP, so the 5/minute limit acts globally. Acceptable for MVP but should derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For for production.
ADMIN_TOKEN is now persisted in backend/.env (good). Consider failing fast if it is missing rather than generating an ephemeral one via secrets.token_urlsafe.

Public /api/posts and /api/case-studies sort by 'published_at' descending; drafts may have null published_at and are excluded via status filter, which is correct. Index on (status, published_at) recommended for scale.
PostCreate is reused for PUT (admin_update_post) - good DRY - but allows changing slug freely; ensure intentional. _unique_slug excludes the current id so no collision risk.

admin_create_post/update_post bypasses Post.created_at/updated_at defaults when constructing via Post(...) which is fine, but update path manually sets updated_at - ensure consistency if Post model evolves.
Frontend Admin.jsx is large (~1300+ lines). Worth splitting into separate components: LeadsPanel, PostsPanel, CaseStudiesPanel, Editor.
test_credentials: Admin password 'mir-admin-2025' verified working; static token 'mir-admin-static-token-d7f3a91b5e6c4a8f' present in backend/.env and returned on successful login.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests cleaned up after themselves (lead, posts, case-studies prefixed TEST_*). Frontend UI test created one lead + one post + one case study and deleted them at the end. Rate-limit test left 5+ /api/leads rejections in the limiter window. Six pre-existing leads remain (from prior iterations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_mir_api_v2.py -v. Tests for /api/leads & /api/admin/login rate-limit are prefixed test_zz_ so they run last (slowapi limiter is 5/min, in-memory). Frontend testids in Admin: admin-tab-{leads,insights,case-studies}, admin-{post,case-study}-{new,row-<id>,edit-<id>,delete-<id>,table}, admin-editor-{title,slug,category,read-time,client,excerpt,cover,outcomes,content,preview,save-draft,publish,cancel}, admin-lead-{view,delete,status}-<id>, admin-drawer-{status,message,notes,save-notes}, admin-leads-{search,status-filter}. After delete in editor/list a confirm AlertDialog opens; click button with text 'Delete'.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs blocked the review checklist.
